semanticCore: Wiping out, destroying, depriving of blessing, erasing, or moon decline
quranicRelevance: Depriving interest of blessing (Yamhaq - 'yamhaqu Allahu ar-riba'), and wiping out disbelievers ('yamhaqa').

keyDerivations.meaning: To wipe out, destroy, deprive of blessings, erase, diminish, or render void

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَمْحَقُ اللَّهُ الرِّبَا وَيُرْبِي الصَّدَقَاتِ (2:276): God deprives interest/usury of all blessing (erasing its real economic growth) and increases charity.
discardedNoise: The crescent moon entering eclipse/invisible stage (Mahaq).
summaryEn: The root represents destruction and depletion of blessing (Mahq), stating that interest-based wealth is deprived of true growth, and promising the wiping out of deniers.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الاستئصال الكلي والذهاب بالبركة والبرء (محق)، مقرراً إبطال وإتلاف أرباح الربا وعقابه المالي، ودحر قوى الكفر.
rawText: لَيَالِى المُحَاقِ : see دَأْدَآءٌ and الدَّعْجَآءُ. مَحَقَ 1 , aor. ﹷ, inf. n. مَحْقٌ, He rendered a thing deficient, and deprived it of its blessing, or increase: or [he annihilated, annulled, or obliterated, it;] he did away with it wholly, so that no trace thereof remained. (Msb.) === أَعْمَدُ مِنْ كَيْلٍ 🡻مُحِّقَ, or مُحِقَ: see عَمَدَ. مَحَّقَ 2 see 1.
